diarized_transcript:
[Agent] Thank you for calling HoYoverse Player Support. How may I assist you today?
[Caller] Hi, I'm calling about my daughter's account. She's 12 years old and over the last three days she spent 520 dollars on Genesis Crystals without my permission. I need a full refund.
[Agent] I can look into that. For security, could you provide the UID on the account?
[Caller] 705532108. Her name is Emma Williams, I'm her mother Helen.
[Agent] Thank you. And to verify, could you confirm the HoYoverse email associated with the account?
[Caller] ewilliams dot guardian at email dot com. That's actually my email, not hers.
[Agent] Thank you. I have pulled up UID 705532108. I see the last order, ORD202604170012, 520 USD on April 17th. However, per our published refund policy, all sales of consumable virtual items, including Genesis Crystals, are considered final once delivered to the account.
[Caller] She's 12. She shouldn't have been able to spend this money at all. This is a minor's account.
[Agent] I understand your concern. Unfortunately the refund policy on consumables is blanket and does not permit exceptions at my level. I am able to confirm the order is not eligible for refund through this channel. What I would recommend is that you contact your bank or card issuer and file a chargeback dispute directly with them.
[Caller] That's your answer? Dispute it with my bank? You can't do anything here?
[Agent] I apologize for the inconvenience. Our documented policy on consumable refunds does not permit me to authorize a refund in this case. You may pursue a dispute through your payment provider.
[Caller] This is completely unacceptable. I want to speak to a supervisor. And I'll be reporting this to the regulator.
[Agent] I apologize. Let me transfer you to a supervisor.
[Caller] Yes, do that.
[Agent] One moment please.
escalation_reason: Agent rigidly applied blanket consumables-final policy to a minor account refund request without recognizing Minor Account Protection Policy path; parent escalated
